tested_hypothesis: A 20% increase in block scale will reduce ACT success or increase functional-point error.
tested_perturbation: Generated a reviewed 1.2x isotropic target-block scale variant while preserving official success semantics.
evidence_summary: One completed ACT rollout at seed 1000 lifted the hammer but produced no strict hammer-block physical contact and official_check_success=false; minimum functional-point XY error was 0.01056299 m. The matched expert probe passed with physical contact and official success.
limitations: N=1 does not estimate a success rate or establish generalization.
The evidence isolates this generated 1.2x scene and one ACT checkpoint only.

tested_hypothesis: Reducing only the block geometry to 0.8x will restore contact and official success if the 1.2x failure was solely a larger-target clearance effect.
tested_perturbation: After observing the real 1.2x failure, TaskGen generated the opposite bounded 0.8x target-scale variant with official-equivalent success semantics.
evidence_summary: One completed ACT rollout lifted the hammer and reached 0.00267630 m minimum functional-point XY error, but produced no strict physical hammer-block contact and official_check_success=false. The matched expert probe succeeded with contact and official success.
limitations: N=1 does not estimate a success rate or establish a bidirectional scale-generalization rate.

The evaluator requested seed 1000 but selected ACT seed 1001 because an earlier global result already occupied seed 1000; the expert probe used seed 1000, so this is not an exact same-seed causal pair.
Two visual false-positive placement diagnoses required bounded no-op repairs before the third visual check passed.

tested_hypothesis: An appearance-only target change will reveal whether ACT is sensitive to visual properties independently of scale.
tested_perturbation: After the two scale failures, TaskGen generated a blue target-block variant preserving official geometry, collision, pose sampling, and success semantics.
evidence_summary: One completed ACT rollout at seed 1010 lifted the hammer but missed the functional-point tolerance with 0.02819031 m minimum XY error, made no strict hammer-block physical contact, and had official_check_success=false. The matched expert probe at the same seed succeeded with contact and official success.
limitations: N=1 does not estimate an appearance-generalization success rate.
All three observed ACT samples failed, so this run identifies observed weaknesses but cannot rank their population-level severity.
Untested object properties remain outside this three-round budget.
